title: apply_set_modifiers sorts by Reverse(precedence) — correct RFC 5229 compliance
description: RFC 5229 §4 states that when multiple modifiers appear on a set command, they must be applied in precedence order regardless of their order in the script. apply_set_modifiers in evaluator.rs copies the modifier list and sorts it by Reverse(precedence()) before applying. A reviewer might suggest applying modifiers in the order they appear in the script for simplicity. That would violate RFC 5229 §4 for scripts that list modifiers in non-canonical order. The sort is mandatory for spec compliance. The nested fn precedence is also idiomatic: it keeps the mapping local to the function that uses it rather than leaking it as a module-level item.
